question_title: Will South Korea sign arms export deals worth >$5bn with any country between July 1 and August 31, 2026?
summary: The question requires a NEW binding single-country ROK arms deal >$5B announced within July 1 – Aug 31, 2026. The Poland K2 second contract ($6.5B) was signed August 1, 2025 — before publication with no explicit start date, so it does not count (forward-looking interpretation). As of July 10, 2026, no >$5B single-country deal is publicly scheduled for the window, and ROK just lost both of its largest near-term prospects: Poland's Orka submarines (to Saab, June 29) and Canada's CPSP submarines (to TKMS, July 7). Most active deals (Saudi/UAE Cheongung-II ~$3.2B, Poland Chunmoo ~$4B) are sub-$5B. The main YES pathway is a K2 third executive contract with Poland, which has a late-summer signing tendency (2022, 2025) but no confirmed 2026 timing and may be delayed by the shift to Polish domestic K2PL production. Weighting the base rate against a thin near-term pipeline but a real-if-uncertain K2 pathway plus high overall export momentum yields ~13%.

factors.description: Base rate: single-country ROK arms deals >$5B are rare (~2-3 over ~48 months), implying ~0.10-0.15 per 2-month window
No confirmed >$5B ROK deal scheduled for the window; most active deals are sub-$5B (Cheongung-II ~$3.2B, Chunmoo ~$4B)
ROK just lost both major submarine competitions (Poland Orka to Saab, Canada CPSP to TKMS), removing the two largest near-term >$5B prospects
K2 third executive contract with Poland is genuinely in the pipeline with an August signing tendency (2022, 2025) and would exceed $5B, but timing is unconfirmed and possibly delayed
Very high ROK export momentum (backlog >$72B, 2026 target $37.7B) creates a tail chance of a surprise large signing

reasoning:
**Forecast: 13% probability (NO likely)**

The question asks whether South Korea will sign a *new* single-country arms export deal worth >$5B between July 1 and August 31, 2026.

Key reasoning:
- The prominent $6.5B Poland K2 tank contract was signed **August 1, 2025** — before the question window and before publication, so it doesn't count.
- As of July 10, 2026, **no >$5B single-country deal is publicly scheduled** for the window.
- South Korea just **lost both of its biggest near-term prospects**: Poland's Orka submarines (to Sweden's Saab, June 29) and Canada's submarine program (to Germany's TKMS, July 7).
- Most active deals in the pipeline (Saudi/UAE Cheongung-II ~$3.2B, Poland Chunmoo ~$4B) fall **below the $5B threshold**.
- The main upside pathway is a K2 third executive contract with Poland — which has a late-summer signing tendency but no confirmed 2026 timing.

Base rate (~12%) plus a real-but-uncertain K2 pathway, offset by the loss of the two largest prospects, lands at **13%**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after June 30, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, the total worth of arms export deals between the Republic of Korea (ROK) and any single country exceeds $5 billion USD, according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). In order to qualify, a deal has to be a binding agreement for a ROK party to sell military equipment or weapons to a counterparty of another country and has to be announced or disclosed by a ROK party.

factors.description: Base rate: single ROK arms deals >$5B with one country are rare events — roughly 2-3 such single-contract announcements over the last ~4 years (Poland Aug 2022, Poland Aug 2025), implying ~0.10-0.15 per random 2-month window
No confirmed >$5B ROK deal is publicly scheduled for the July-Aug 2026 window; most active near-term deals (Saudi/UAE Cheongung-II ~$3.2B, Chunmoo ~$4B) are sub-$5B
ROK just lost both major submarine competitions (Poland Orka to Saab June 29; Canada CPSP to TKMS July 7), removing the two largest near-term >$5B prospects
K2 third executive contract with Poland is genuinely in the pipeline (1000-tank framework) and Poland historically signs K2 deals in August (2022, 2025); would be >$5B if it lands in window
Very high ROK export momentum (backlog >$72B, 2026 target $37.7B) and many active large prospects create tail chance of a surprise large signing

input.anchor: ~12% base rate: 2-3 single >$5B ROK deals over ~48 months = ~0.10-0.13 per 2-month window
input.assessment: Question needs a NEW binding single-country ROK arms deal >$5B announced within July 1 - Aug 31, 2026. The pre-publication Poland K2 second contract ($6.5B, Aug 1 2025) does NOT count (Case 2 - forward-looking). No confirmed deal is scheduled. Two biggest prospects (Poland/Canada subs) both just lost. The main YES pathway is a K2 third executive contract, which has a late-summer signing tendency but no confirmed 2026 timing and may be delayed by the shift to Polish domestic production. Middle East Cheongung-II follow-ons are active but sub-$5B. Base rate ~12-15%, K2-third possibility adds some upside but timing uncertain. Settling ~13%.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether a K2 third executive contract or a surprise Middle East mega-deal lands specifically in this 2-month window. Timing of the K2 third contract is the dominant swing factor.
input.tool_audit: research() sub-agent returned excellent, specific, well-sourced findings on the exact pipeline state as of July 10 2026. Both queries succeeded with detailed data points. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective here — it directly identified the pre-publication trap (K2 second contract was Aug 2025, not 2026) and mapped the forward pipeline precisely. This is a case where good backward-looking research nearly resolves the forecast. No friction; the scaffolding fit the question well.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All five factors are grounded in the research trace, which I verified directly.

Factor-by-factor check:
- F1 (base rate ~0.10-0.15): Trace confirms Poland's July/Aug 2022 first K2 executive contract and Aug 1, 2025 K2 second contract ($6.5B). 2-3 single >$5B deals over ~48 months is a reasonable, sourced reference class. Grounded.
- F2 (no scheduled >$5B deal; Saudi/UAE sub-$5B): Trace key_facts explicitly state no confirmed >$5B single-country ROK contract is scheduled for the window; Saudi Cheongung-II $3.2B and Chunmoo ~$4B are confirmed sub-$5B. Grounded.
- F3 (lost both submarine competitions): Trace confirms Orka → Saab (June 29, 2026, ~$4.8B) and Canada CPSP → TKMS (July 7, 2026); Hanwha lost both. Grounded and correctly directional.
- F4 (K2 third contract, August cadence, >$5B if lands): Trace confirms the 1,000-tank framework, that a third executive contract is anticipated with no confirmed date, and August signings in 2022 and 2025. The +0.9 logit with confidence 0.5 correctly reflects both the real pathway and its timing uncertainty (plus the drag from the shift to Polish domestic K2PL production). Grounded.
- F5 (backlog/momentum tail): Backlog and 2026 export target supported by trace; small +0.3 logit appropriate.

Logit signs all match descriptions — no wrong-direction factor. No double-counting (each factor draws on a distinct evidence cluster). No hallucination.

Pre-publication event handling is correct and is the crux of this question. The Aug 1, 2025 K2 second contract ($6.5B) predates published_at (July 10, 2026) with no explicit start date before publication — Case 2. The agent explicitly refused to treat it as resolving the question and built a forward-looking estimate, using it only as base-rate evidence. Exactly right.

Resolution criteria were provided and the agent engages the specific terms (single country, >$5B, binding agreement, ROK-disclosed, July 1–Aug 31 window). No resolution misalignment.

Counterargument is strong, not a token gesture: it constructs a coherent alternative story (K2 August cadence + Middle East Cheongung-II aggregation) and names concrete disconfirming evidence that would move it to 25-40%. Confidence self-assessment (0.75) is calibrated to the acknowledged timing uncertainty.

Tool audit narrative is consistent with ground truth (14 calls, 0 errors); the agent does not falsely claim zero failures where failures existed.

Worldview check: the two research entries (b19a6093, 2fb0eec7) match this question's research; no contradicting or double-counted forecast exists on this quantity.

Independent probability: base ~12%, minus the loss of the two largest sub prospects, plus a real-but-uncertain K2-third pathway and mild peak-season tail — I land in ~11-16%. The agent's 13% sits squarely within my range and is driven by the evidence rather than round-number hedging. No meaningful divergence. Approve.
